summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is an ionic bond?

An electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions is correct. This is a key concept in Ionic Bonds.

What is the difference between an ionic and covalent bond?

Ionic involves electron transfer; covalent involves electron sharing. This is an important concept in Ionic Bonds.

What is the typical state of ionic compounds at room temperature?

Solid. This is an important concept in Ionic Bonds.

What is Coulomb's law relevant to in ionic bonding?

It describes the force between charged particles. This is an important concept in Ionic Bonds.

What is a monovalent ion?

An ion with a charge of +1 or -1. This is an important concept in Ionic Bonds.

What is the electron configuration of Cl⁻?

Same as argon (1s²2s²2p⁶3s²3p⁶). This is an important concept in Ionic Bonds.

What is a divalent ion?

An ion with a charge of +2 or -2. This is an important concept in Ionic Bonds.

What is the formula for aluminum sulfate?

Al³⁺ and SO₄²⁻: LCM of 3 and 2 = 6. Need 2 Al (2×3=+6) and 3 SO₄ (3×2=6). Formula: Al₂(SO₄)₃.

What is the name for Fe₂O₃?

Fe₂O₃: 3 O²⁻ = −6; 2 Fe = +6; each Fe = +3 = Fe³⁺. Fe has variable charge → use Roman numeral: iron(III) oxide.
